name: Cetalkonium
description: Cetalkonium is more commonly present in the salt form, cetalkonium chloride. It is a a quaternary ammonium compound of the alkyl-benzyldimethylammonium chloride family with 16C-long chains. It is an active ingredient in pharmaceutical products to treat mouth ulcers or dental sores due to its considerable antibacterial actions. It is also used as an excipient in topical or ophthalmic drug products due to its amphiphilic property and cationic charge. The formation of cetalkonium chloride cationic oil-in-water nanoemulsions is shown to improve the precorneal residence time.
